headline: Sudan FLOOR CONFIRMATION at 0.0 / Critical — floor sustained. Two milestone primary-source events documented May 11-12: (1) UN Human Rights Chief Türk (May 11) reported armed drones caused 80%+ of civilian deaths in Sudan war in first 4 months of 2026 — at least 880 killed by drones alone. (2) Banjul Joint Declaration on Sudan (May 12), adopted by the African Commission on Human and Peoples' Rights' Joint Fact-Finding Mission and the UN Independent International Fact-Finding Mission for Sudan during the 87th ACHPR session (Banjul, The Gambia, April 24 - May 20). The declaration represents the FIRST COORDINATED AU + UN formal condemnation of both warring parties — characterizing violations as 'widespread and systematic' and identifying RSF abuses as 'particularly widespread and systematic.' This is a milestone in international documentation. New conduct categories: 'drone-warfare as primary civilian-kill mechanism' and 'joint AU-UN fact-finding condemnation.' Composite remains 0.0; conduct documentation appended; floor-exit evidentiary burden compounded substantially.

conduct_documentation.summary: Floor-entity conduct documentation update with TWO major primary-source events May 11-12: UN HRC drone-warfare casualty data and Banjul Joint Declaration first-coordinated AU+UN fact-finding condemnation. No score change (floor); conduct categories appended.
new_conduct_categories_this_cycle: Drone-warfare as primary civilian-kill mechanism (May 11 UN HRC report): drones caused 80%+ of civilian deaths in Sudan war first 4 months 2026; at least 880 killed by drones alone in that period; conducted by both SAF and RSF.

Joint AU-UN fact-finding condemnation (May 12 Banjul Joint Declaration): first coordinated AU + UN formal condemnation of both warring parties; violations characterized as 'widespread and systematic' overall; RSF violations specifically 'particularly widespread and systematic'; recommendations for prompt independent investigations; call for ceasefire, justice, civilian rule.
conduct_documentation.international_documentation_milestone: The Banjul Joint Declaration is qualitatively distinct from prior single-mechanism findings: it represents the first time the African Commission on Human and Peoples' Rights and the UN Independent International Fact-Finding Mission have jointly adopted a coordinated condemnation. This raises the floor-exit evidentiary burden because: (a) any future Sudan engagement with rehabilitation would require both AU and UN re-engagement, not single-mechanism re-engagement; (b) the coordinated finding strengthens any future ICC or universal-jurisdiction case; (c) the documentation pattern now spans the OHCHR Feb 2026 El-Fasher genocidal-campaign finding (carry-over) + the Banjul joint declaration + the UN drone-casualty data — three independent institutional findings within 4 months.
conduct_documentation.drone_warfare_dimension_analysis: The drone-as-primary-civilian-kill-mechanism finding is structurally significant beyond Sudan-specific scoring: it establishes a documented pattern of drone-warfare-driven civilian harm at high scale (880+ in 4 months) attributed to both warring parties. For Sudan floor-record purposes: civilian-targeting via drone is a category-level breach distinct from artillery/ground-operations breach; the 80%+ figure means drone-warfare is THE dominant kill mechanism. This compounds the El-Fasher genocidal-campaign finding (Feb 2026: 6,000+ killed in 3 days post-RSF capture) — which was ground-operations based.
carry_over_conduct_categories: OHCHR / UN Fact-Finding Mission Feb 2026 finding: RSF genocidal campaign at El-Fasher confirmed; three Genocide Convention prohibited acts; 6,000+ killings in 3 days post-capture (carry-over)
17% of $2.8bn 2026 humanitarian appeal funded (carry-over update — sustained funding gap)
Daily drone strikes in Kordofan targeting markets, health facilities, schools (carry-over context for May 11 UN finding)
conduct_documentation.floor_exit_criteria_impact: Compound primary-source documentation (Feb 2026 OHCHR + May 11 UN drone data + May 12 Banjul AU-UN joint declaration) raises floor-exit evidentiary burden substantially. Floor exit would now require: (a) cessation of drone strikes on civilians by both parties, (b) cooperation with AU + UN fact-finding mechanisms (not just one), (c) accountability process for genocide-convention findings. None of these are in trajectory.

key_evidence: May 12 2026 (in-window): Banjul Joint Declaration on Sudan adopted by ACHPR Joint Fact-Finding Mission + UN IIFFMS during 87th ACHPR session. First coordinated AU + UN formal condemnation. Violations 'widespread and systematic'; RSF violations 'particularly widespread and systematic.' Call for prompt independent investigations.

May 11 2026 (in-window): UN Human Rights Chief Türk reports armed drones caused 80%+ of civilian deaths in Sudan war in first 4 months 2026; at least 880 killed by drones alone in that period. Strikes conducted by both Sudan army and RSF.

Feb 2026 (anchor carry-over): OHCHR / UN Fact-Finding Mission report — El-Fasher evidence reveals genocidal campaign by RSF targeting non-Arab populations; three Genocide Convention prohibited acts confirmed; 6,000+ killings in 3 days post-capture.
Cumulative humanitarian context: 30M+ in humanitarian need; 61.7% acutely food insecure; 17% of $2.8bn 2026 humanitarian appeal funded; 150,000+ total killed.
HRW World Report 2026 Sudan: comprehensive SAF-RSF civil war documentation (anchor).

evidence.source: UN News — Armed drones leading cause of civilian death in Sudan war: UN rights chief
evidence.finding: 80%+ of civilian deaths in Sudan war first 4 months 2026 caused by armed drones; at least 880 killed by drones alone; strikes by both SAF and RSF.

evidence.source: ACHPR — Banjul Joint Declaration on Sudan
evidence.finding: First coordinated AU + UN formal condemnation of both warring parties. Violations 'widespread and systematic'; RSF violations 'particularly widespread and systematic.' Call for prompt independent investigations and ceasefire/civilian-rule.
Joint declaration text and adoption context confirmation.

evidence.source: Euronews — Sudan drone strikes killed at least 880 civilians between January and April, UN says
evidence.finding: Independent media corroboration of UN HRC drone-casualty data.

evidence.source: Dabanga Radio TV — AU and UN missions warn Sudan faces deepening atrocities
evidence.finding: Independent regional media documentation of AU-UN joint warning; call for ceasefire/justice/civilian rule.

evidence.source: OHCHR Feb 2026 — Sudan El-Fasher genocidal campaign
evidence.finding: Anchor carry-over: UN Fact-Finding Mission confirms RSF genocidal campaign; three Genocide Convention prohibited acts.

math_check.validator_check: Floor maintained. New evidence does not affect composite but compounds floor-exit evidentiary burden.

negativeSignals: Drones cause 80%+ civilian deaths (880+ in 4 months); first coordinated AU + UN formal condemnation; RSF violations 'particularly widespread and systematic'; El-Fasher genocidal-campaign finding (Feb 2026, carry-over); 30M+ in humanitarian need; 61.7% acutely food insecure; 17% appeal funded; 65%+ hospitals closed; daily drone strikes in Kordofan targeting markets, health facilities, schools.
positiveSignals: None internal to the state. External: AU + UN fact-finding mechanisms functioning and producing coordinated documentation — this is structural-international-system positive (relevant to Banjul as an institutional event), but does not reflect Sudan state-actor compassion behavior.
sector_context: Countries floor-cluster (Sudan, South Sudan, Russia, Myanmar, Israel). Sudan distinguished this cycle by milestone-class international-documentation events: Banjul Joint Declaration is the first coordinated AU + UN fact-finding condemnation in the active rotation; UN drone-casualty data is the first quantified primary-cause-of-civilian-death finding. Cross-cluster collateral: drone-warfare-as-primary-kill-mechanism category may apply to other conflict-zone floor entities in future cycles.
open_actions_resolved: Banjul Joint Declaration documented as new conduct-category event (joint AU-UN fact-finding condemnation)
UN HRC drone-casualty data documented as new conduct-category event (drone-warfare as primary civilian-kill mechanism)
open_actions_carried: Watch: any state response to Banjul Joint Declaration
Watch: drone-warfare cessation or continuation pattern
Watch: humanitarian appeal funding progress (17% baseline)
Floor exit criteria require: cessation of drone strikes on civilians + cooperation with AU + UN fact-finding mechanisms + accountability process for genocide-convention findings — none in trajectory
disclaimer: This assessment is based on publicly available information and does not constitute a formal Compassion Benchmark Certified Assessment.
